resources.string: Scan for and connect to your radar, dashcam and eBike over Bluetooth.
Post the silent service notification and any ride alerts.
Draw the radar overlay on top of whatever\'s on screen. Without this, alerts still play but you won\'t see the overlay.
Used once per ride to set your front and radar lights by local sunset. Skip it and sunset is estimated for London.
Grant Bluetooth permissions in Settings to start scanning
Turn on close-pass counting in Settings → Alerts to count them here and keep a ride history.
The overlay shows its battery and flags when it stops broadcasting.
System permissions so the app can find your devices, post a status notification, and draw the overlay.
Bluetooth: finds and connects your radar, dashcam and eBike
Notifications: shows ride status and alerts
Location (approx, optional): sets your front and radar lights by local sunset - never tracks you
Send ride and battery data to HA for logging, dashboards, and pre-ride reminders.
Set up the URL and token now.
Skip this step. The app works without HA.
You can change this later from Settings → Home Assistant.
In HA: Profile → Security → Long-lived access tokens.
Enter URL and token to continue.
You can add Home Assistant any time from Settings → Home Assistant.
Sends battery, light mode, ride times and ride stats (incl. close passes) to your HA
Goes only to your Home Assistant - never to the developer
Your HA address and token are encrypted on your phone
Pairing happens in Android\'s Bluetooth settings, not here. Put the radar in pair mode, then tap Open Bluetooth settings.
Not paired yet. The pairing screen is in Android\'s Bluetooth settings.
After pairing, press back to return here.
You said you don\'t have one
Change your mind any time from Settings → Dashcam.
Pick the dashcam you ride with to enable off-warnings.
Got a Bluetooth dashcam? The overlay shows its battery and flags when it stops broadcasting.
You can come back to this later in %1$s.
Reads your paired-device list to find the radar and dashcam
Saves which dashcam you ride with, on your phone
Nothing from pairing is sent anywhere
More features in Settings -> Experimental once you\'re set up.
You can pair the radar later from Bluetooth settings.
Show your eBike battery and connection status while you ride - works alongside the Bosch Flow app.
eBike battery and status on the home screen
Close-pass beeps stay on during a slow climb
Beep timing scales with your wheel speed
Skip this step. The app works without it.
You can change this later from Settings -> eBike.
We can read your eBike - no action needed.
Open Bosch Flow and keep it running while you ride.
Install Bosch Flow to get your eBike\'s live data.
You can finish now and set this up later.
Don\'t have a Bosch eBike? Go back
Couldn\'t open Flow. Install it from the Play Store and try again.
Reads your bike\'s battery, speed and pedalling - read-only, never sends anything to your bike
Stays on your phone, saved to the ride log
If Home Assistant is set up, ride and battery info is sent there
Rear-radar alerts, on screen and with sound.
Every dependency the app ships with
What stays on the phone, what doesn\'t
Not affiliated with Garmin or Bosch
Features still being tested. May be jittery or change without notice.
Render each vehicle 1 s into the future - see where overtakers are heading, not just where they are. Can look jittery in noisy traffic.

Pan beeps and urgent cues to the threat\'s side via stereo. Works on phone speakers in landscape (rotates with the mount) and on stereo headphones (BT, BLE, wired, USB, hearing aid). Portrait plays mono.
Flip channels if directional cues land in the wrong ear (rare device-class quirk or earbuds worn on the wrong side).
Beep volume for approach alerts. 0 silences audio; the overlay still flashes.
Start beeping when a vehicle is this close. Vehicles farther away appear on the overlay but stay silent. Scaled by bike speed when adaptive alerts are on.
Sound the urgent warning when a vehicle bears down while you\'re riding slowly, not only when you\'re stopped.
Farthest vehicle drawn on the overlay. Beyond this, approaching traffic is ignored on screen.
Fade the overlay so a map or navigation app underneath stays readable. \"Off\" leaves the overlay at its full look.
Scale amber / red thresholds by your bike speed: more sensitive when stopped, less when cruising.
Once the radar\'s been out of range this long, retries slow to the interval below so Bluetooth can rest while the bike is parked.
How often to retry the connection while the radar is idled out. Longer values save battery overnight; shorter values pick up the radar faster when you come back.
Without a Bosch eBike: keep the rear-radar warning on screen until the radar reconnects, instead of hiding it after a few seconds.
Show an amber warning beside the rider when any paired device drops below this level.
Show \'RADAR 12%\' or \'DASHCAM 8%\' on screen instead of a silent warning tint.
Count overtakes inside the clearance below - on the home screen and in your ride history.
Count overtakes inside the clearance below - on the home screen, in your ride history, and sent to Home Assistant.
Only count a pass when the minimum lateral clearance drops below this distance.
Detector ignores stationary-rider situations (red lights, pushing the bike).
Roughly %1$d km/h of relative approach speed.
Shuts down radar, overlay, and HA updates until you start them again. No auto-start on reboot. Use Pause for a quiet hour instead.
Start it again from the home screen.
The radar, overlay, and Home Assistant updates stop until you start them again from the home screen - including after a reboot. Use Pause if you only need a quiet hour.
Your rear radar - the core of the app.
Put the radar in pair mode, then pair it in Android\'s Bluetooth settings.
Pick any paired device to use as this bike\'s radar.
Opens Bluetooth settings. Forget the old radar there first.
Publishing to Home Assistant via MQTT
Live eBike data is on. Leave Bosch Flow running in the background while you ride.
Shows your eBike battery and connection status on the home screen, while Bosch Flow runs in the background on this phone.
Needs a Bosch Smart System eBike and the Bosch Flow app.
I have a Bosch Smart System eBike now
Tap to turn on live data.
Turn on to show eBike battery and status on the home screen.
Battery and status - shown while Bosch Flow runs in the background.
Battery and ride status from your bike.
Open Flow and ride - data appears here automatically.
Buzz your watch if you walk away with the bike still unlocked.
Allowed. The reminder buzzes your watch even with DND on.
Tap to let the reminder buzz your watch through DND.
Live data needs Flow running in the background while you ride.
Shows eBike battery and connection status on the home screen
Keeps close-pass beeps firing on a slow climb
Scales beep timing to your wheel speed
Reads battery, speed and pedalling - read-only, never sends anything to your bike
Stays on your phone, saved to the ride log
Ride and battery info goes to Home Assistant only if you set it up
Full detail in the Privacy screen.
Couldn\'t open Flow. Install it from the Play Store and try again.
Turn this on if you want to track a Bluetooth dashcam alongside the radar.
Warn on overlay when dashcam is off
Show a camera-off icon next to the rider when no Vue advert is seen.
Phone vibrates + beeps when you walk out of range with the dashcam still powered up (camera, light, or both).
Currently allowed. Alarm will sound even with DND on.
Tap to allow this alarm to play through DND in system settings.
How long the dashcam must be unreachable before the alarm fires.
Bike Radar collects no telemetry and runs no analytics. Your data goes only to the Home Assistant you set up - nowhere else.
Your settings (alert volume, alert distance, paired-radar status, dashcam preferences, and similar) live in Android\'s app-private storage and never leave the device.

Your Home Assistant URL and token are encrypted at rest with a hardware-backed AES-256/GCM key from the Android Keystore. The key never leaves the secure element, so raw filesystem access (e.g. `adb pull`) recovers only ciphertext.

A short ride history (ride times, distance, overtake and close-pass counts, and how close and how fast the closest passes were - no location, no route) is kept in app-private storage for the Ride history screen. The oldest entries are dropped after 200 rides; nothing is shared.

Capture logging is off by default. Turn it on (Debug screen) and the app records each ride - radar packets, BLE notifications, eBike data from Bosch Flow, phone-battery samples; no location. Logs stay on your phone (gzipped to save space) and are shared only if you tap Share.
If the app crashes, a report (app version, thread, stack trace - no ride data, no location) is kept in app-private storage and shared only if you tap Share on the Debug screen.

A small connection log (when the radar, dashcam and the app\'s service connected, disconnected or failed to connect - device names and times only, no ride data, no location) is kept in app-private storage for troubleshooting on the Debug screen.
What goes to your Home Assistant

If you set up HA, the app publishes over MQTT, straight to your HA URL - nothing routed through the developer or any third party:
• radar and dashcam battery
• front-light mode
• ride start/end events
• an end-of-ride summary (distance, close-pass counts, closing speeds, clearances)
• a per-event close-pass log, if you turn it on
You can stop publishing at any time by tapping Clear HA configuration in Settings → Home Assistant. Stored credentials are removed from the encrypted store immediately.

Pairing happens in Android\'s system Bluetooth flow, not in this app. The app reads your bonded-device list to identify the radar, the dashcam, and your Bosch eBike. Bluetooth permissions are declared with `usesPermissionFlags=\"neverForLocation\"`, so Bluetooth scanning never derives location.
The only network destination is the HA URL you provide. No analytics endpoints, crash reporters, ad networks, or third-party libraries that send data out.

If your HA URL points outside your home network, the app requires HTTPS and refuses to send your token in cleartext. Plain HTTP is accepted only for LAN destinations: private IPv4 ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x), loopback, IPv6 unique-local and link-local, and `.local` / `.lan` hostnames.

The app uses these Android permissions:
• BLUETOOTH_SCAN, BLUETOOTH_CONNECT: find and connect your radar, dashcam, eBike
• POST_NOTIFICATIONS: ride status, alerts and the post-ride summary
• SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW: draw the overlay over your map
• FOREGROUND_SERVICE: keep running during rides
• ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION: read once per ride to set your lights by local sunset; never tracks you, never leaves the phone

Screen capture (off by default) asks for MediaProjection each time and records the whole screen, including whatever's behind the overlay.
The app is open-source under GPL-3.0-or-later. The full source code is at github.com/partymola/android-bike-radar-overlay. Verify any of the above by reading the code.
Used once per ride to compute accurate sunrise/sunset for the light auto-modes. Skip it and sunset is estimated for London.
Sets the radar tail light on connect and at sunset
The radar can\'t confirm the switch - if in doubt, glance at the tail light.
Sets the dashcam light on connect and at sunset
Bike Radar is itself GPL-3.0-or-later. Every third-party library it depends on is listed below with its licence. All licences are compatible with GPL-3.0.
Pair a new device in Android Settings
Likely matches are paired devices whose name looks like a cycling dashcam or that have advertised battery this session.
Each ride lands here a few minutes after the radar powers down. Everything stays on your phone.

Off by default. Records every radar packet, BLE notify and eBike snapshot with exact timing - useful for bug reports, but ride-tracking-grade data kept on the phone. Takes effect on the next radar connection (the current ride\'s log finishes either way).
Capture the screen every minute while the radar overlay is active. Saved to the app\'s files dir under screenshots/.
Writes ebike_unk lines for unmapped records. Toggle each bike state during a capture to pin lock/light/charger IDs, then turn off.
Logs radar_2f14 / radar_2f12 lines from the radar control service. Cycle the tail-light modes (button or vendor app) to pin the night/day encoding, then turn off.
This log records the exact time of every radar packet. Anyone you share it with can work out when and where you rode, and how often vehicles passed close. Only share with people you trust.
This permanently removes all %1$d capture log(s) from this phone. It can\'t be undone. The current ride\'s log (if any) is not in this list and is kept.
Radar pairing was removed. Re-pair in Bluetooth settings to resume.
Alerts when the front camera/light mode could not be applied.
Reminds you if you walk off without locking the eBike.
A quiet recap of each ride once the radar powers down.
Alerts when the radar turns off but the dashcam is still broadcasting from the bike.
Radar is off but the dashcam is still on your bike. Battery draining, easy to forget.
Capturing screenshots while overlay is active
